news.title: Finance Ministry: GST collections cross ₹1.87 lakh crore in April 2025
news.summary: Gross GST revenue collections for April 2025 stood at ₹1,87,346 crore, marking a 12.4% year-on-year growth. CGST accounted for ₹33,100 crore, SGST for ₹41,200 crore, IGST for ₹98,200 crore, and Cess for ₹14,846 crore. The number of GST returns filed hit a record high, indicating improved compliance.
news.background: GST (Goods & Services Tax) replaced multiple indirect taxes and was implemented on 1 July 2017 under the 101st Constitutional Amendment Act, 2016. It is governed by the GST Council (Article 279A) chaired by the Union Finance Minister. GST has a 4-tier rate structure: 5%, 12%, 18%, and 28%. Essential items are either exempt or taxed at 0%. Gold and precious stones attract 3%.

keyTerms.definition: Goods & Services Tax — a unified indirect tax subsuming Central Excise, Service Tax, VAT, and others. Implemented 1 July 2017.
Central GST — collected by the Central Government on intra-state supply of goods/services.
State GST — collected by the State Government on intra-state supply.
Integrated GST — collected by Centre on inter-state supply and imports. Later apportioned between Centre and states.
Constitutional body under Article 279A. Chaired by Union Finance Minister with state finance ministers as members. Recommends GST rates.
Constitutional amendment that introduced GST (Articles 246A, 269A, 279A). Passed in 2016, effective 2017.

news.whyItMatters: GST is one of the most asked Economy topics in SSC CGL. Memorise: implementation date, constitutional article, council composition, rate slabs, and the difference between CGST/SGST/IGST.
news.examPoint: GST: 1 July 2017, 101st Amendment, Article 279A (Council). Rate slabs: 5%, 12%, 18%, 28%. Council chaired by Union Finance Minister.

news.title: Health Ministry expands Mission Indradhanush 5.0 to cover all districts
news.summary: The Ministry of Health & Family Welfare launched Intensified Mission Indradhanush (IMI) 5.0 covering all 764 districts. The mission targets full immunisation of children under 2 years and pregnant women who were missed under routine immunisation. The Universal Immunisation Programme (UIP) now covers vaccines against 12 diseases including measles-rubella, rotavirus, pneumococcal disease, and Japanese encephalitis.
news.background: Mission Indradhanush was launched on 25 December 2014 to strengthen and re-energise the Universal Immunisation Programme (UIP). India's UIP is one of the world's largest public health programmes. The Intensified Mission Indradhanush (IMI) focuses on districts with low immunisation coverage. India achieved elimination of maternal and neonatal tetanus in 2015 and introduced several new vaccines under UIP.

keyTerms.definition: Launched 25 Dec 2014 to accelerate full immunisation coverage. Named after the 7 colours of the rainbow (Indradhanush), originally covering 7 vaccine-preventable diseases.
India's national immunisation programme, one of the largest in the world, covering 12 vaccine-preventable diseases.
Tuberculosis (BCG), Diphtheria, Pertussis, Tetanus, Polio, Hepatitis B, Measles-Rubella, Japanese Encephalitis, Pneumococcal disease, Rotavirus diarrhoea, Haemophilus influenzae type b.
An accelerated version targeting low-coverage areas to achieve 90% full immunisation coverage.

news.whyItMatters: Health schemes are frequently asked in SSC CGL. Expect questions on: Mission Indradhanush launch year, number of diseases covered under UIP, and the implementing ministry.
news.examPoint: Mission Indradhanush: 25 Dec 2014. UIP: 12 diseases. Ministry of Health & Family Welfare. Target: 90% full immunisation.

news.title: National Award for e-Governance 2025 conferred for innovative districts
news.summary: The Department of Administrative Reforms and Public Grievances (DARPG) conferred the National Awards for e-Governance 2025 during the 27th National Conference. Awards were given in categories including Government Process Re-engineering, District-level Innovation, and Citizen-Centric Services. The awards recognise exemplary use of ICT in government administration.
news.background: The National e-Governance Awards are conferred by DARPG under the Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions. They are given annually since 2004 to recognise and promote excellence in e-Governance initiatives at central, state, and district levels. India's e-Governance framework is guided by the Digital India programme (launched 1 July 2015) and the National e-Governance Plan (NeGP).

keyTerms.definition: Department of Administrative Reforms & Public Grievances — under Ministry of Personnel, Public Grievances & Pensions. Oversees administrative reform and the CPGRAMS portal.
Flagship programme launched 1 July 2015 by PM Modi to transform India into a digitally empowered society. Three pillars: Digital Infrastructure, Digital Governance, Digital Empowerment.
Centralised Public Grievance Redress and Monitoring System — online platform for citizens to lodge grievances with the Central Government.
National e-Governance Plan — umbrella plan for e-governance projects including DigiLocker, UMANG, e-Hospital, etc.

news.whyItMatters: E-Governance and Digital India initiatives are important for SSC CGL Polity. Questions often ask about specific portals (UMANG, DigiLocker), launch dates, and implementing departments.
news.examPoint: Digital India: 1 July 2015. DARPG under Ministry of Personnel. Key portals: CPGRAMS, UMANG, DigiLocker. e-Gov Awards since 2004.

news.title: RBI Monetary Policy: Repo Rate kept at 6.50% by MPC
news.summary: The six-member Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) of the Reserve Bank of India, headed by Governor Shaktikanta Das, decided unanimously to keep the policy repo rate unchanged at 6.50%. The standing deposit facility (SDF) rate remains at 6.25% and the marginal standing facility (MSF) rate at 6.75%. The MPC retained its stance as 'withdrawal of accommodation' to ensure inflation progressively aligns with the 4% target while supporting growth.
news.background: The RBI's MPC meets six times a year (bi-monthly) to decide the benchmark interest rate. The repo rate is the rate at which commercial banks borrow from the RBI. A higher repo rate makes borrowing expensive, reducing money supply and curbing inflation. India's retail inflation (CPI) target is 4% with a tolerance band of ±2% (i.e., 2%–6%), as set by the Government of India under the flexible inflation targeting framework introduced in 2016.

keyTerms.definition: The rate at which RBI lends short-term funds to commercial banks against government securities. Currently 6.50%.
The rate at which RBI borrows from commercial banks. It is the floor of the LAF corridor.
A 6-member statutory body (3 RBI + 3 Government nominees) established under Section 45ZB of the RBI Act, 1934. Decisions are taken by majority vote; the RBI Governor has a casting vote.
Percentage of a bank's total deposits that must be kept with RBI as liquid cash. Currently 4.5%. Earns no interest.
Percentage of net demand and time liabilities that banks must maintain in gold, cash, or government securities. Currently 18%.
A facility for banks to park excess funds with RBI without collateral. Introduced in April 2022. Acts as the floor of the LAF corridor.

news.whyItMatters: RBI policy rates (Repo, Reverse Repo, CRR, SLR, MSF, Bank Rate) are among the most frequently asked topics in SSC CGL General Awareness. Questions often ask: 'Who is the current RBI Governor?', 'What is the current repo rate?', 'How many members are in the MPC?', and 'Which section of the RBI Act established MPC?'.
news.examPoint: Memorise: Repo = 6.50%, SDF = 6.25%, MSF = 6.75%, CRR = 4.5%, SLR = 18%. MPC has 6 members under RBI Act Section 45ZB.

news.title: Cabinet approves PM-KISAN 18th instalment — ₹20,000 crore released
news.summary: The Union Cabinet approved the release of the 18th instalment of the Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi (PM-KISAN) scheme, transferring ₹2,000 each to approximately 9.5 crore farmer families across India. The total disbursement under this instalment is approximately ₹20,000 crore, directly credited to beneficiaries' bank accounts via Direct Benefit Transfer (DBT).
news.background: PM-KISAN was launched on 24 February 2019. Under this scheme, eligible farmer families receive ₹6,000 per year in three equal instalments of ₹2,000 each. The scheme is 100% funded by the Central Government and is implemented by the Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare. It uses the Aadhaar-seeded bank account system for DBT. As of 2025, over ₹3 lakh crore has been disbursed to more than 11 crore registered farmers since inception.

keyTerms.definition: Pradhan Mantri Kisan Samman Nidhi — a Central Sector scheme providing ₹6,000/year in 3 instalments to small and marginal farmer families. Launched: 24 Feb 2019.
A Government of India initiative to transfer subsidies and benefits directly to the bank accounts of beneficiaries. Launched on 1 January 2013 to reduce leakages.
A scheme 100% funded by the Central Government (unlike Centrally Sponsored Schemes which have cost-sharing with states).
The process of linking Aadhaar number with a bank account to enable identity verification for DBT.

news.whyItMatters: PM-KISAN is one of the most asked government schemes in SSC CGL. Questions test: launch year, amount per instalment, implementing ministry, and the DBT mechanism. Also asked: difference between Central Sector and Centrally Sponsored Schemes.
news.examPoint: PM-KISAN: ₹6,000/year in 3 instalments of ₹2,000. Central Sector scheme. Ministry of Agriculture & Farmers' Welfare. Launched 24 Feb 2019.

news.title: ISRO successfully launches PSLV-C58 carrying XPoSat satellite
news.summary: The Indian Space Research Organisation (ISRO) successfully launched the PSLV-C58 mission from the Satish Dhawan Space Centre (SDSC-SHAR) in Sriharikota, Andhra Pradesh. The primary payload was XPoSat (X-ray Polarimeter Satellite), India's first dedicated polarimetry mission to study cosmic X-ray sources. XPoSat was placed in a 650 km Low Earth Orbit (LEO). The PSLV-C58 also carried 10 payloads on the POEM-3 (PSLV Orbital Experimental Module) platform.
news.background: PSLV (Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle) is ISRO's workhorse rocket, operational since 1993. It has completed 60+ missions with a remarkable success rate. XPoSat is only the world's second X-ray polarimetry satellite after NASA's IXPE (launched 2021). ISRO was established on 15 August 1969 and is headquartered in Bengaluru. The current Chairman of ISRO is S. Somanath. India became the 4th country to soft-land on the Moon with Chandrayaan-3 in August 2023.

keyTerms.definition: Polar Satellite Launch Vehicle — ISRO's reliable 4-stage launch vehicle. Can carry up to 1,750 kg to Sun-Synchronous Polar Orbit (600 km).
Geosynchronous Satellite Launch Vehicle — heavier rocket with cryogenic upper stage for communication satellites in Geosynchronous Transfer Orbit.
X-ray Polarimeter Satellite — India's first mission to study polarisation of X-rays from celestial sources like black holes, neutron stars, and pulsars.
Satish Dhawan Space Centre, Sriharikota, Andhra Pradesh — India's primary spaceport, located on Sriharikota island between Pulicat Lake and Bay of Bengal.
PSLV Orbital Experimental Module — converts the spent 4th stage of PSLV into a stabilised platform for in-orbit experiments.

news.whyItMatters: ISRO missions are a staple in SSC CGL Science & Tech. Questions ask: launch vehicle used, satellite name and purpose, launch site, ISRO chairman, and India's space firsts (Chandrayaan-3, Aditya-L1, Gaganyaan).
news.examPoint: ISRO HQ: Bengaluru. Chairman: S. Somanath. Launch site: SDSC-SHAR, Sriharikota, AP. PSLV = polar orbits, GSLV = geosynchronous orbits.

news.title: India signs MoU with France on green hydrogen and solar energy cooperation
news.summary: India and France signed a Memorandum of Understanding (MoU) to strengthen bilateral cooperation in green hydrogen production, solar energy, and energy storage technologies. The MoU was signed under the International Solar Alliance (ISA) framework. India aims to produce 5 million tonnes of green hydrogen annually by 2030 under the National Green Hydrogen Mission (NGHM), approved in January 2023 with an outlay of ₹19,744 crore.
news.background: The International Solar Alliance (ISA) was jointly launched by India and France at COP21 in Paris on 30 November 2015. ISA is headquartered in Gurugram, Haryana. It has 116 member countries and aims to mobilise $1 trillion in solar investment by 2030. India's National Green Hydrogen Mission targets making India a global hub for green hydrogen by using renewable energy for electrolysis-based hydrogen production.

keyTerms.definition: An inter-governmental treaty-based organisation co-founded by India and France in 2015 at COP21. HQ: Gurugram, Haryana. Director General: Dr Ajay Mathur.
Hydrogen produced by splitting water (electrolysis) using renewable energy sources. Unlike grey hydrogen (from natural gas), it produces zero carbon emissions.
Launched in January 2023 with ₹19,744 crore outlay. Target: 5 million tonnes/year green hydrogen production by 2030.
Annual UN Climate Change Conference under UNFCCC. COP21 (Paris, 2015) produced the Paris Agreement to limit global warming to 1.5°C.

news.whyItMatters: ISA, green hydrogen, and India's bilateral agreements are recurring topics. SSC asks: 'Where is ISA HQ?', 'Who co-founded ISA?', 'What is the green hydrogen target?', and 'Which COP produced the Paris Agreement?'.
news.examPoint: ISA: India + France, HQ Gurugram, launched at COP21 (Paris 2015). Green Hydrogen Mission: ₹19,744 cr, target 5 MT/year by 2030.

news.title: Government launches Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) for central employees
news.summary: The Union Cabinet approved the Unified Pension Scheme (UPS) for central government employees, effective from 1 April 2025. Under UPS, employees retiring after 25 years of service will receive an assured pension of 50% of the average basic pay of the last 12 months. For those with 10–25 years of service, the pension will be proportionate. A minimum pension of ₹10,000/month is guaranteed for employees with at least 10 years of service. The Government contribution increases to 18.5% (from 14% under NPS).
news.background: The Old Pension Scheme (OPS) was replaced by the National Pension System (NPS) for employees joining after 1 January 2004. NPS is a defined-contribution scheme (market-linked returns, no guaranteed pension) regulated by the Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority (PFRDA). UPS is a hybrid model that restores assured pension benefits while retaining some NPS features.

keyTerms.definition: Assured pension scheme for central govt employees. 50% of avg basic pay (last 12 months) after 25 years. Effective 1 April 2025.
Defined-contribution pension scheme launched 1 Jan 2004 for new govt employees. Market-linked returns, regulated by PFRDA.
Defined-benefit pension: 50% of last drawn basic pay as pension. Discontinued for new joiners post-2004.
Pension Fund Regulatory and Development Authority — statutory body established under PFRDA Act, 2013 to regulate NPS and pension sector.

news.whyItMatters: Pension-related questions frequently appear in SSC CGL Polity & Economy. Expect questions on: NPS vs OPS, PFRDA's role, UPS eligibility criteria, and government contribution percentages.
news.examPoint: UPS: 50% of avg basic pay after 25 yrs, min ₹10,000/month, govt contributes 18.5%. NPS regulated by PFRDA (est. 2013). OPS ended for post-2004 joiners.

vocab.meaning: Provide evidence to support or prove the truth of
vocab.sentence: He could not substantiate his claims.
She abhors any form of dishonesty.

vocab.meaning: Make something greater by adding to it
vocab.sentence: He took a second job to augment his income.

vocab.meaning: Large or heavy and therefore difficult to use
vocab.sentence: The old procedure was slow and cumbersome.
His health began to deteriorate rapidly.

vocab.meaning: Serving as a desirable model; very good
vocab.sentence: She received an award for her exemplary service.
His gregarious nature made him popular.
The team was jubilant after the win.
